signal_title: Slovenia's Draft Law on Cannabis for Limited Personal Use
risk_rationale: The draft bill raises child protection concerns which could impact TikTok's youth safety policies, particularly in relation to content moderation and age assurance. However, the bill is still in draft form and lacks a specified effective date, indicating a medium risk that can be addressed within a quarter.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Minimum age for TikTok service is 13+.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Child protection concerns related to cannabis content.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The draft law may necessitate stricter content moderation policies to prevent exposure of cannabis-related content to minors.

compliance_gaps.baseline_policy_id: 4.1 Community rulebook and effective policy dates
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Compliance with Digital Services Act regarding child protection.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Potential need to update community guidelines to address cannabis-related content in compliance with child protection standards.

remediations.title: Enhance Content Moderation for Cannabis-Related Content
remediations.description: Implement stricter content moderation rules to identify and restrict cannabis-related content from being accessible to users under 18.
acceptance_criteria: Cannabis-related content is flagged and restricted for users under 18.
Updated community guidelines are published and communicated to users.

remediations.title: Update Age Assurance Mechanisms
remediations.description: Enhance age assurance mechanisms to ensure accurate age verification, particularly in regions where cannabis use is legalized.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification processes are updated to reflect new regulatory requirements.
Increased accuracy in detecting underage users.

business_impact: Potential reputational risk and regulatory scrutiny if cannabis-related content is not adequately moderated, leading to non-compliance with child protection standards.
recommended_actions: Monitor the progression of the draft bill and adjust compliance strategies accordingly.
Engage with EU regulators to understand specific compliance expectations.
Prepare communication plans to inform users of any changes to content policies.
